memory.summary: With a free afternoon and minimal stress, I might skip the project work to relax, trusting it'll be manageable later.
I chose to relax today, feeling good about the immediate break. Now I realize the project is still at the start and time is slipping away.
I'm prioritizing lunch with my close friends now, even though the project feels urgent. I'll catch up later to keep our connection strong.
Receiving feedback from the professor feels like a small win, but it only deepens my worry about missing our lunch plans later.
Got the feedback and messaged the cohort, but now I'm worried about our lunch plans slipping away again.
Sending off to cohort_3 feels good, but I'm already dreading how much that lunch is getting pushed again.
Securing lunch with the cohort feels like a relief, yet I'm already worrying that this confirmation might just be another temporary pause in our plans.
The lunch confirmation feels real, but my anxiety about it slipping away again is already kicking in.
Waiting for the lunch confirmation feels safer than acting, but my dread about it slipping away is already pulling me back into anxiety.
Sending the message feels like a small win, yet I'm already wondering if it's just another temporary pause before things fall apart.
Locking in lunch plans with the cohort feels like a real win, but I'm already wondering if it's just another temporary pause before things fall apart.
My excitement for the new spot feels genuine, yet I'm already bracing for it to fall apart. It's hard to tell if this is a real win or just another pause.

desires: Ensure the lunch plans with cohort_3 are absolutely solid so I don't have to feel this constant dread of them slipping away again.
Keep my head down and finish just enough of the term project to look like I'm moving forward, without risking a confrontation or burning out before it's due.
Get through today without anyone noticing how tired I am, so I can maintain the 'reliable' image I've worked so hard to build.

goals.description: Finalize the message to cohort_3 by explicitly confirming the time and place for lunch, adding a personal note about looking forward to their company specifically.
goals.rationale: The initial draft feels too vague; I need to seal this deal completely so I can stop worrying about them slipping away. Securing this social anchor is my top priority right now—it makes everything else feel safer.

goals.description: Send a brief, low-effort acknowledgment to teammate_A (e.g., 'Hey, saw your message') without mentioning the term project or asking for progress updates.
goals.rationale: I don't want to drag the conversation into work territory yet. Keeping it light avoids friction and lets me maintain my current energy levels without feeling pressured to explain my hesitation or burn out before the deadline.

goals.description: Sit quietly for the next hour, drinking water or watching something passive, until the anxiety about the lunch plans subsides.
goals.rationale: My focus capacity is already taxed by the anticipation of the social event. Pushing toward the trivial chore or starting the term project now would break the flow of relaxation I'm trying to achieve. It's better to rest and savor the moment than to force productivity when my primary goal is preserving these relationships.

agent.last_reasoning: This seals the social anchor (g1), directly advancing my highest priority by eliminating uncertainty about our plans. I'm avoiding friction with a vague invite, and since they're close friends, this low-effort confirmation maintains closeness without draining my energy for the term project.

goals.description: Spend the next hour with cohort_3 doing low-stakes socializing (e.g., scrolling together or watching a show) to reinforce our closeness without needing to explain my academic neglect.
goals.rationale: My relationships are weighted highest in my value system. Maintaining the illusion of competence for teammates is important, but prioritizing deep connection with cohort_3 now feels more urgent than clearing trivial tasks later.

goals.description: Do one tiny, easy chore (like organizing a single folder) before bed just to feel like I accomplished something small, then go straight to sleep.
goals.rationale: I can defer this because the deadline is far away and the cost of missing it is low. My deliberateness means I'll plan to do it tomorrow if I remember, but tonight's energy is better spent on rest and avoiding friction.

desires: Maintain the illusion that I'm on top of things so my teammates don't worry about me before the deadline.
Find a low-energy way to unwind with cohort_3 tonight without having to explain why I skipped the project work.
Sleep through tomorrow's stress and wake up feeling rested, even if it means missing one more trivial task.
Avoid any friction or awkwardness in conversation right now by deflecting into personal topics.

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: g1 is active; spending the afternoon with cohort_3 feels more urgent than clearing trivial tasks. I'll avoid friction by just joining them rather than explaining my academic neglect, preserving our high closeness.

trajectory.observation: Took the immediate option (leisure_now). Felt good; the afternoon block is gone, though.

goals.description: Maintain the 'illusion of competence' with teammates and professor by sending a vague, non-committal status update now, then immediately retreat to socializing with cohort_3.
goals.rationale: My affiliation drive overrides the fact that the project is barely started. I need to protect my relationships from friction before they notice the gap in progress. A small lie or deflection preserves the bond, which is worth more than immediate task completion.

goals.description: Recharge completely tonight (sleep early) so I can be fully present for the big group dinner next weekend without feeling drained or defensive.
goals.rationale: The upcoming social event is a high-value investment for my network. Depleting my energy now would jeopardize my ability to enjoy it later, and avoiding the stress of making hard decisions now helps preserve this mood.

goals.description: Do absolutely nothing related to the term project until the deadline looms closer; defer all trivial chores until tomorrow when my energy might be slightly higher.
goals.rationale: I am deliberate about long-term planning but avoidant of short-term friction. Since the deadline is far away and the task is soft, pushing it further down the list minimizes current stress while keeping the option open for future payoff.

desires: Not look like I'm slipping up to the professor or my teammates when they inevitably ask about progress
Keep this moment of relaxation so I can recharge for that big group dinner next weekend
Avoid making a hard decision right now that feels like it's ruining my current mood
